meta_title: For Students: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: API Integration Guide to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Master API integration and conquer debugging hurdles. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development workflow.
content.answer_block: Debugging API failures requires a systematic approach: isolate the endpoint, validate the payload, and inspect the response headers. By using Claude Code to analyze stack traces and Antigravity to simulate edge cases, you can pinpoint integration errors in seconds, transforming frustrating roadblocks into predictable, manageable development milestones.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the terminal glows with a wall of red text, and the '400 Bad Request' error feels like a personal insult. You are staring at your first production-ready API integration, but the data isn't flowing. This is the 'debugging wall'—the exact moment where amateur coders quit and future senior engineers are forged.

But here is the secret: you don't need to be a wizard to fix this. You need a better process. By leveraging AI-driven tooling like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can treat your codebase like a laboratory. Let’s strip away the confusion and turn that error log into a roadmap for your next big breakthrough.

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Before debugging, you must ensure the request structure matches the documentation. Misaligned schemas are the #1 cause of silent integration failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this API schema and create a validation script that checks my outgoing payload against the expected JSON structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read schema.json && claude ask 'Does this payload structure violate the provided schema?'

steps.title: Isolate the Endpoint
steps.description: Stop debugging the entire app. Isolate the specific API call to see if the issue is in your request logic or the external server's response.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimal reproduction script using axios to test only this specific endpoint with dummy data.

steps.title: Analyze Response Headers
steps.description: Errors are often hidden in headers. Use AI to parse the status codes and rate-limiting information returned by the server.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain this API response header set and identify why I might be receiving a 429 error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze these response headers: [paste headers here]'

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases
steps.description: Use Antigravity to mock server failures to see how your code handles timeouts or malformed responses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock server response that simulates a 500 internal server error to test my application's error handling logic.

steps.title: Refactor for Resiliency
steps.description: Implement robust error handling and retry logic based on the insights gained from your testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this API call to include exponential backoff and structured error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Refactor the fetch logic to include exponential backoff'

pro_tips: Always log the full request URL and body to a local file before it hits the network; it makes comparison debugging trivial.
Use environment variables for API keys; hardcoding is the fastest way to leak secrets and break CI/CD pipelines.
Treat every API error as a data point; if you see a pattern in the logs, you’ve found the root cause of the bug.
Keep your mock responses in a separate folder to allow for quick regression testing as your app grows.
When using AI to debug, always provide the 'expected' vs 'actual' behavior to drastically increase the quality of the AI's suggestions.
content.next_steps: Now that you have fixed your integration, deploy a small dashboard that visualizes the API response data to complete your portfolio project.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the mindset to stop debugging in the dark. Your journey from student to AI-powered builder starts with the code you ship today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
